text.title: SEO Audit Runbook Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules — one per audit phase — that govern a repeatable annual SEO audit. Skipping any phase ships a partial audit that misleads the prioritisation backlog.

rule.statement: Phase 1 (Crawl) MUST produce: (a) full URL inventory from a desktop crawler (Screaming Frog / Sitebulb / Lumar) capped at the live sitemap, (b) status-code distribution, (c) orphan-URL list (in crawl, not in sitemap; in sitemap, not in crawl), (d) duplicate-title / duplicate-description list. Missing any artefact = phase REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without a current crawl, every subsequent phase analyses stale data. Orphan + duplicate detection is the cheapest 80/20 win an audit produces.

rule.statement: Phase 2 (On-Page) MUST sample at least the top-100-traffic URLs (by GSC clicks) and audit each against `on-page-seo-checklist-2026`. Findings MUST be filed with URL + checklist-item-id + severity (P0/P1/P2). Sampling fewer than top-100 = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Top-traffic URLs concentrate revenue; on-page issues there have outsize fix-impact. Auditing low-traffic URLs first is a common time-sink.

rule.statement: Phase 3 (Content Decay) MUST compare GSC clicks/impressions for the audit window vs the prior equivalent window, flag URLs with ≥30% decline that still rank in top-20, and assign each to one of {refresh, merge, delete, redirect}. Decay phase WITHOUT decision per URL = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Decay is the highest ROI for established sites and the easiest to skip. Forcing a per-URL decision turns a list into work.

rule.statement: Phase 4 (Links) MUST audit: (a) internal-link graph for orphan / under-linked top-of-funnel pages, (b) external backlink growth/loss over the window from Ahrefs/SEMrush/Majestic, (c) toxic-backlink candidates flagged for disavow review (if any). All three sub-audits required.
rule.rationale: Internal-link audits alone miss external link-loss attacks; external-only audits miss internal cluster gaps. Both are required for a defensible audit.

rule.statement: Phase 5 (Tech-SEO) MUST verify: Core Web Vitals (CrUX or PageSpeed Insights field data, not lab), indexability (robots.txt, X-Robots-Tag, canonical chains), structured-data validation (Rich Results Test for top templates), hreflang for multilingual sites, and mobile parity. Field data, not lab data, is the floor.
rule.rationale: Lab CWV (Lighthouse synthetic) misleads on real-user performance; Google ranks on field data. Auditors who use lab numbers lose credibility with engineering.

rule.statement: Phase 6 (Report) MUST emit a single document with: executive summary (≤ 1 page), findings backlog ranked by ICE or RICE score, recommended sequencing (quick wins first 30 days, structural fixes next 60), and a sign-off block (auditor + recipient + date). Reports without a ranked backlog = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Unranked findings backlogs rot in Notion. Ranking + sequencing is what turns the audit from a deliverable into actual work.

example.title: Finding entry
example.text: id: F-014 phase: content-decay url: /blog/best-crm-2024 observation: clicks ↓ 58% vs prior 12mo, still ranks #6 for primary query decision: refresh proposed_action: update with 2026 entities + new SERP-features table ICE: I=8 C=8 E=7 → score=448 owner: @content-lead due: sprint+2
